verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically located on the mitochondrial inner membrane. However, well-replicated evidence documents an ectopic cell-surface pool (ecto-ATP synthase / eATP synthase) on tumor cells (leukemia, liver, lung, breast, prostate carcinoma), vascular endothelial cells, and hepatocytes. The mitochondrial complex is trafficked to the plasma membrane via microtubule/DRP1/KIF5B-mediated mitochondrial membrane fusion events. Cell-surface ATP5B has been targeted by monoclonal antibodies, RNA aptamers, and mimetic cytotoxic peptides in multiple cancer contexts. Surface display is upregulated in oncogenic/activated states rather than being a stable baseline PM pool, fitting cell_state_induced contextual surface biology.
